questions.q: In which year was the Ghadar Party founded?
questions.explanation: The Ghadar Party was founded in 1913 by Indian emigrants on the west coast of North America.

questions.q: On which continent was the Ghadar Party originally founded?
questions.explanation: It was founded among Indian immigrants on the west coast of North America (the United States and Canada).

questions.q: What was the main goal of the Ghadar Party?
options: To overthrow British rule through armed revolution
questions.explanation: The party aimed to overthrow British rule in India through an armed revolution.

questions.q: The word 'Ghadar' means what?
questions.explanation: 'Ghadar' (or Ghadr) is an Urdu word meaning revolt or mutiny, reflecting the party's revolutionary aim.

questions.q: During which war did many Ghadar members return to India to organize an uprising?
questions.explanation: Many members returned to India during the First World War hoping to spark a mass uprising against British rule.

questions.q: Most of the early Ghadar Party members were emigrants from which Indian province?
questions.explanation: A large majority of Ghadar members were Punjabi immigrants, many of them Sikhs, settled on the North American west coast.

questions.q: The Ghadar Party published a newspaper to spread its message. What was it called?
questions.explanation: The party published the weekly newspaper 'Ghadar' to spread revolutionary ideas among Indians abroad.

questions.q: The Ghadar movement is an example of Indian nationalism organized where?
questions.explanation: It was a notable example of revolutionary nationalism organized abroad among overseas Indian communities.

questions.q: The Ghadar Party sought freedom for India by what kind of means?
questions.explanation: Unlike the Moderates, the Ghadar Party advocated armed revolution to win independence.

questions.q: Indian emigrants who founded the Ghadar Party were mostly working as what in North America?
questions.explanation: Many were labourers and farmers facing racial discrimination, which fuelled their revolutionary nationalism.

questions.q: In which American city was the headquarters of the Ghadar Party established?
questions.explanation: The Ghadar Party set up its headquarters, the Yugantar Ashram, in San Francisco.

questions.q: Who is generally regarded as the founding president of the Ghadar Party?
questions.explanation: Sohan Singh Bhakna was the founding president of the Ghadar Party in 1913.

questions.q: Which intellectual served as the general secretary and chief ideologue of the early Ghadar Party?
questions.explanation: Lala Har Dayal, a scholar based in the United States, was the principal ideologue and organizer of the Ghadar Party.

questions.q: The Ghadar Party's headquarters in San Francisco was known by what name?
questions.explanation: Its San Francisco headquarters and press were known as the Yugantar Ashram.

questions.q: Which young revolutionary, hanged in 1915, became one of the most celebrated martyrs of the Ghadar movement?
questions.explanation: Kartar Singh Sarabha was executed in 1915 at the age of about 19 and became an inspiring Ghadar martyr.

questions.q: The Ghadar Party's planned 1915 uprising in India is associated with which conspiracy trials?
questions.explanation: The failed 1915 plot led to the first Lahore Conspiracy Case, in which many Ghadarites were tried.

questions.q: Which incident involving a ship denied entry to Canada in 1914 greatly galvanized Ghadar sentiment?
questions.explanation: The 1914 Komagata Maru incident, in which Indian passengers were refused entry to Canada, intensified Ghadar feeling.

questions.q: The Ghadar Party originally drew many recruits from Indian communities in which two North American regions?
questions.explanation: Indian immigrants in California (USA) and British Columbia (Canada) formed the party's main recruiting base.

questions.q: During the First World War, the Ghadar Party sought military and financial assistance from which European power?
questions.explanation: The Ghadarites sought support from Germany, Britain's wartime enemy, in the so-called Hindu-German Conspiracy.

questions.q: Which experienced Bengal revolutionary came to Punjab to help coordinate the planned Ghadar uprising of 1915?
questions.explanation: Rash Behari Bose helped lead the planning for the February 1915 uprising before escaping to Japan.

questions.q: The planned Ghadar uprising of 1915 ultimately failed mainly because of what?
questions.explanation: British intelligence, aided by infiltrators and informers, uncovered the plot and made mass arrests, dooming the rising.

questions.q: The Ghadar newspaper was published in multiple Indian languages including Urdu and which other?
questions.explanation: The 'Ghadar' weekly was printed in Urdu and Gurmukhi (Punjabi), among other languages, to reach its diaspora readers.

questions.q: What was the original full name of the organization that became known as the Ghadar Party?
options: The Pacific Coast Hindustan Association (Hindi Association)
questions.explanation: It was first organized as the Pacific Coast Hindustan Association (Hindi Association) before becoming known by its paper, Ghadar.

questions.q: On approximately which date was the abortive Ghadar uprising planned to break out in Punjab in 1915?
questions.explanation: The uprising was planned for 21 February 1915 but was betrayed and advanced/aborted after the plans leaked.

questions.q: Lala Har Dayal fled the United States in 1914 to avoid arrest and went to which country?
questions.explanation: Har Dayal left the US in 1914 after his arrest by American authorities and eventually made his way to Germany.

questions.q: Which Indian revolutionary, later linked to Ghadar circles, was an early figure agitating among Indians in North America before 1913?
questions.explanation: Taraknath Das was an early activist among Indian immigrants in North America who helped lay groundwork for the movement.

questions.q: The Ghadar conspirators' attempt to subvert Indian troops during WWI included plans to incite mutiny in which 1915 garrison?
questions.explanation: Ghadar-inspired agitation contributed to unrest among Indian sepoys, with the 1915 Singapore Mutiny part of the wider wartime ferment.

questions.q: In the United States, several Ghadar leaders and German agents were prosecuted in 1917-1918 in which famous trial?
options: The San Francisco Hindu-German Conspiracy Trial
questions.explanation: The Hindu-German Conspiracy Trial in San Francisco (1917-18) prosecuted Ghadarites and German agents for plotting against British India from US soil.

questions.q: Sohan Singh Bhakna, the Ghadar Party's first president, hailed from which district of Punjab?
questions.explanation: Sohan Singh Bhakna was from the village of Bhakna in the Amritsar district of Punjab.

questions.q: How old was Kartar Singh Sarabha when he was hanged for his role in the Ghadar conspiracy in 1915?
questions.explanation: Kartar Singh Sarabha was around 19 when executed in November 1915, making him a youthful icon of the movement.
